reason: fence opened on a bullet line (`- ```calc`): mldoc splits an empty bullet `- ` then a Src; lsdoc keeps the line as one bullet. Adversarial; not how Logseq writes code-in-bullet (it nests as a child block). Revisit if a real graph needs it.

nested ordered list via markdown indentation (`1. a\n   1. nested`): mldoc folds the nested item into the parent's sub-items (normalizes to a `raw` node); lsdoc emits two flat items. Logseq nests via outline bullets, not markdown indentation. Revisit if a real graph needs it.

fence opened after a bullet prefix (`- ```\ncode\n````): mldoc splits an empty bullet `- ` then a Src; lsdoc keeps the ``` on the bullet line. Same class as c047 — Logseq nests code under a bullet as a child block, not on the bullet line.

fence (with language) opened after a bullet prefix (`- ``` clj :results\n…`): same class as c047/m096 — mldoc emits empty bullet + Src, lsdoc keeps it on the bullet line. Adversarial outline form not produced by Logseq.

markdown blockquote opened after a bullet prefix (`  - > line3`): mldoc splits an empty bullet `  - ` then a Quote (lazy-joining the next `> ` line across indentation); lsdoc keeps `> line3` as the bullet title. Same family as c047/b021 (a block construct starting after the bullet prefix). Logseq nests quotes as child blocks.

same as m114 (quote opened after a bullet prefix, deeper nesting): mldoc emits empty bullet + lazy-joined Quote; lsdoc keeps the quote on the bullet line. Adversarial outline indentation, not produced by Logseq.
same as m114/m115 plus a tab-indented property/fence tail: mldoc's bullet-prefix block split + lazy continuation diverge from lsdoc's per-line outline model. Adversarial; not produced by Logseq.

markdown definition list (`term\n: definition`): mldoc emits a List whose item carries a `name` (the term) and the definition as content; lsdoc emits a paragraph. Def-list syntax is niche in Logseq (it uses outline bullets / `key:: value`), and the list-item `name` field is outside the modeled projection. Revisit if a real graph needs it.

LaTeX named entity (`\Delta G`): mldoc maps `\Delta` to an `Entity` node via a ~2k-entry LaTeX entity table; lsdoc renders an unknown `\word` as the bare letters (correct for non-entities). Porting the full entity table is disproportionate for a math/org-flavored construct rare in Logseq Markdown.
LaTeX named entity with `{}` (`\Delta{}G`): same as m054 — `\Delta` is a table entity in mldoc; lsdoc emits the bare letters. Entity table not ported (niche in Logseq Markdown).

LaTeX environment block (`\begin{equation}…\end{equation}`): mldoc emits a `Latex_Environment` block; lsdoc emits a paragraph. Latex_Environment is not modeled in the projection and is a math/org construct rare in Logseq Markdown.
